_comment: Curated modifier-class table. Each known trailing modifier token (the Modifier field of a decomposed ModelRef) is tagged IDENTITY or ATTRIBUTE. IDENTITY modifiers distinguish a genuinely different model artifact (different weights/behavior) and are PART of the entity key — they render in the '{...}' segment. ATTRIBUTE modifiers describe a per-instance presentation or runtime knob that does NOT change identity and are EXCLUDED from the key — they render in the '[...]' segment. ClassifyModifier consults 'global' first-pass and 'family_overrides' as a per-family override that BEATS the global classification (mirroring the existing per-family member-guard pattern in modifiers.json): the same token can be identity-bearing for one family and a mere speed/runtime attribute for another (gpt-4-turbo: turbo=IDENTITY vs glm-5-turbo: turbo=ATTRIBUTE). Any token absent from BOTH layers defaults to IDENTITY (the fail-safe over-split — never silently collapse two artifacts into one entity). The global default for the AMBIGUOUS tokens (turbo/fast/chat/pro/precision) is therefore IDENTITY; per-family overrides demote specific ones to ATTRIBUTE where curation has established the token is a speed/runtime tier for that family. mini/pro/flash STAY IDENTITY-class modifiers this epoch (a dedicated size/param axis is deferred to GH#9 — NO modifier->variant reclassification here). All keys lowercase; matching is case-insensitive at runtime.
